title: narcissus tazetta subsp. italicus
intro: Narcissus tazetta subsp. italicus, also known as the Italian daffodil or daffodil narcissus, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Amaryllidaceae family. This plant contains alkaloids, such as galantamine and lycorine, which exhibit medicinal properties, including antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic activities. The Italian daffodil offers various health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues and coughs due to its expectorant properties, as well as helping to reduce anxiety and stress. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures, teas, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
